274 species in Merrimack County, New Hampshire, US

Search results reflect the entire county of the location you enter.

  • Best Match
  • Family
Black-capped Chickadee

This tiny bird has a short neck and large head, giving it a distinctive, rather spherical body shape. It also has a long, narrow tail and a short bill a bit thicker than a warbler’s but thinner than a finch’s.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
Blue Jay

Large crested songbird with broad, rounded tail. Blue Jays are smaller than crows, larger than robins.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
American Goldfinch

A small finch with a short, conical bill; small head; long wings; and short, notched tail.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
American Crow

A large, long-legged, thick-necked bird with a heavy, straight bill. In flight, the wings are fairly broad and rounded with the wingtip feathers spread like fingers. The short tail is rounded or squared off at the end.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
Tufted Titmouse

Tufted Titmice look large among the small birds that come to feeders, an impression that comes from their large head and eye, thick neck, and full bodies. The pointed crest and stout bill help identify titmice even in silhouette.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
Northern Cardinal

The Northern Cardinal is a fairly large, long-tailed songbird with a short, very thick bill and a prominent crest. Cardinals often sit with a hunched-over posture and with the tail pointed straight down.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
American Robin

American Robins are fairly large songbirds with a large, round body, long legs, and fairly long tail. Robins are the largest North American thrushes, and their profile offers a good chance to learn the basic shape of most thrushes. Robins make a good reference point for comparing the size and shape of other birds, too.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
White-breasted Nuthatch

The largest nuthatch, this is still a small bird with a large head and almost no neck. The tail is very short, and the long, narrow bill is straight or slightly upturned.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
Mourning Dove

Plump-bodied and long-tailed, with short legs, small bill, and a head that looks particularly small in comparison to the body. The long, pointed tail is unique among North American doves.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
Downy Woodpecker

Downy Woodpeckers are small versions of the classic woodpecker body plan. They have a straight, chisel-like bill, blocky head, wide shoulders, and straight-backed posture as they lean away from tree limbs and onto their tail feathers. The bill tends to look smaller for the bird’s size than in other woodpeckers.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
Song Sparrow

Song Sparrows are medium-sized and fairly bulky sparrows. For a sparrow, the bill is short and stout and the head fairly rounded. The tail is long and rounded, and the wings are broad.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
Red-eyed Vireo

Red-eyed Vireos are large, chunky vireos with a long, angular head, thick neck, and a strong, long bill with a small but noticeable hook at the tip. The body is stocky and the tail fairly short.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
Gray Catbird

A medium-sized, slender songbird with a long, rounded, black tail and a narrow, straight bill. Catbirds are fairly long legged and have broad, rounded wings.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
Dark-eyed Junco

The Dark-eyed Junco is a medium-sized sparrow with a rounded head, a short, stout bill and a fairly long, conspicuous tail.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
Hairy Woodpecker

A medium-sized woodpecker with a fairly square head, a long, straight, chisel-like bill, and stiff, long tail feathers to lean against on tree trunks. The bill is nearly the same length as the head.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
Eastern Phoebe

The Eastern Phoebe is a plump songbird with a medium-length tail. It appears large-headed for a bird of its size. The head often appears flat on top, but phoebes sometimes raise the feathers up into a peak. Like most small flycatchers, they have short, thin bills used for catching insects.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
Red-winged Blackbird

A stocky, broad-shouldered blackbird with a slender, conical bill and a medium-length tail. Red-winged Blackbirds often show a hump-backed silhouette while perched; males often sit with tail slightly flared.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
Common Yellowthroat

Common Yellowthroats are small songbirds with chunky, rounded heads and medium-length, slightly rounded tails.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
Chipping Sparrow

The Chipping Sparrow is a slender, fairly long-tailed sparrow with a medium-sized bill that is a bit small for a sparrow. Learning the shape of this classic Spizella sparrow is a key step in mastering sparrow identification.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
House Finch

House Finches are small-bodied finches with fairly large beaks and somewhat long, flat heads. The wings are short, making the tail seem long by comparison. Many finches have distinctly notched tails, but the House Finch has a relatively shallow notch in its tail.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
Eastern Kingbird

The Eastern Kingbird is a sturdy, medium-sized songbird with a large head, upright posture, square-tipped tail, and a relatively short, straight bill.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
White-throated Sparrow

The White-throated Sparrow is a large, full-bodied sparrow with a fairly prominent bill, rounded head, long legs, and long, narrow tail.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
European Starling

Starlings are chunky and blackbird-sized, but with short tails and long, slender beaks. In flight their wings are short and pointed, making them look rather like small, four-pointed stars (and giving them their name).

Media Player Error
Update your browser
Eastern Bluebird

The Eastern Bluebird is a small thrush with a big, rounded head, large eye, plump body, and alert posture. The wings are long, but the tail and legs are fairly short. The bill is short and straight.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
Red-bellied Woodpecker

A sleek, round-headed woodpecker, about the same size as a Hairy Woodpecker but without the blocky outlines.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
Mallard

Mallards are large ducks with hefty bodies, rounded heads, and wide, flat bills. Like many “dabbling ducks” the body is long and the tail rides high out of the water, giving a blunt shape. In flight their wings are broad and set back toward the rear.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
Eastern Wood-Pewee

Eastern Wood-Pewees are medium-sized flycatchers with long wings and tails. Like other pewee species, they have short legs, upright posture, and a peaked crown that tends to give the head a triangular shape. Their long wings are an important clue to separate them from Empidonax flycatcher species.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
House Sparrow

House Sparrows aren’t related to other North American sparrows, and they’re differently shaped. House Sparrows are chunkier, fuller in the chest, with a larger, rounded head, shorter tail, and stouter bill than most American sparrows.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
Canada Goose

Canada Geese are big waterbirds with a long neck, large body, large webbed feet, and wide, flat bill.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
Northern House Wren

Small and compact, with a flat head and fairly long, curved beak. Short-winged, often keeping its longish tail either cocked above the line of the body or slightly drooped.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
Cedar Waxwing

The Cedar Waxwing is a medium-sized, sleek bird with a large head, short neck, and short, wide bill. Waxwings have a crest that often lies flat and droops over the back of the head. The wings are broad and pointed, like a starling’s. The tail is fairly short and square-tipped.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
Ovenbird

The Ovenbird is a chunky, larger-than-average warbler, but still smaller than a Song Sparrow. It has a round head, fairly thick bill for a warbler, and a jaunty tail often cocked upward.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
Pine Warbler

Pine Warblers are hefty, long-tailed warblers with stout bills. The tip of the tail usually appears to have a central notch.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
Ruby-throated Hummingbird

The Ruby-throated Hummingbird is a small hummingbird with a slender, slightly downcurved bill and fairly short wings that don’t reach all the way to the tail when the bird is sitting.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
Veery

Veeries are medium-sized thrushes—smaller than an American Robin but similar in shape. They have a plump body, round head, a straight, narrow bill, and fairly long wings and legs.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
Common Grackle

Common Grackles are large, lanky blackbirds with long legs and long tails. The head is flat and the bill is longer than in most blackbirds, with the hint of a downward curve. In flight, the wings appear short in comparison to the tail. Males are slightly larger than females.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
Northern Yellow Warbler

Northern Yellow Warblers are small, evenly proportioned songbirds with medium-length tails and rounded heads. For a warbler, the straight, thin bill is relatively large.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
Red-breasted Nuthatch

A small, compact bird with a sharp expression accentuated by its long, pointed bill. Red-breasted Nuthatches have very short tails and almost no neck; the body is plump or barrel-chested, and the short wings are very broad.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
Chimney Swift

Chimney Swifts are very small birds with slender bodies and very long, narrow, curved wings. They have round heads, short necks, and short, tapered tails. The wide bill is so short that it is hard to see.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
American Redstart

A medium-sized warbler with a relatively wide, flat bill and fairly long, expressive tail. In flight it has a deep chest, slim belly, and long, somewhat club-shaped tail.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
Black-and-white Warbler

Black-and-white Warblers are medium-sized warblers (small songbirds). They have a fairly long, slightly downcurved bill. The head often appears somewhat flat and streamlined, with a short neck. The wings are long and the tail is short.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
Tree Swallow

Tree Swallows are streamlined small songbirds with long, pointed wings and a short, squared or slightly notched tail. Their bills are very short and flat.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
Common Raven

Not just large but massive, with a thick neck, shaggy throat feathers, and a Bowie knife of a beak. In flight, ravens have long, wedge-shaped tails. They're more slender than crows, with longer, narrower wings, and longer, thinner “fingers” at the wingtips.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
American Tree Sparrow

American Tree Sparrows are small, round-headed birds that often fluff out their feathers, making their plump bodies look even chubbier. Among sparrows, they have fairly small bills and long, thin tails.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
Brown Creeper

Brown Creepers are tiny yet lanky songbirds. They have long, spine-tipped tails, slim bodies, and slender, decurved bills.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
Pileated Woodpecker

The Pileated Woodpecker is a very large woodpecker with a long neck and a triangular crest that sweeps off the back of the head. The bill is long and chisel-like, about the length of the head. In flight, the wings are broad and the bird can seem crowlike.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
Eastern Warbling Vireo

Eastern Warbling Vireos are small, chunky songbirds with thick, straight, slightly hooked bills. They are medium-sized for vireos, with a fairly round head and medium-length bill and tail.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
Carolina Wren

The Carolina Wren is a small but chunky bird with a round body and a long tail that it often cocks upward. The head is large with very little neck, and the distinctive bill marks it as a wren: long, slender, and downcurved.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
Rock Pigeon

Larger and plumper than a Mourning Dove, Rock Pigeons are tubby birds with small heads and short legs. Their wings are broad but pointed wings and the tail is wide and rounded.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
Northern Flicker

Flickers are fairly large woodpeckers with a slim, rounded head, slightly downcurved bill, and long, flared tail that tapers to a point.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
Northern Mockingbird

A medium-sized songbird, a bit more slender than a thrush and with a longer tail. Mockingbirds have small heads, a long, thin bill with a hint of a downward curve, and long legs. Their wings are short, rounded, and broad, making the tail seem particularly long in flight.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
Chestnut-sided Warbler

An slim warbler with a relatively long tail that it often holds cocked, or raised above the body line, which makes the tail appear longer still.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
Scarlet Tanager

Scarlet Tanagers are medium-sized songbirds with fairly stocky proportions. They have thick, rounded bills suitable both for catching insects and eating fruit. The head is fairly large and the tail is somewhat short and broad.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
Great Blue Heron

Largest of the North American herons with long legs, a sinuous neck, and thick, daggerlike bill. Head, chest, and wing plumes give a shaggy appearance. In flight, the Great Blue Heron curls its neck into a tight “S” shape; its wings are broad and rounded and its legs trail well beyond the tail.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
Turkey Vulture

Turkey Vultures are large dark birds with long, broad wings. Bigger than other raptors except eagles and condors, they have long "fingers" at their wingtips and long tails that extend past their toe tips in flight. When soaring, Turkey Vultures hold their wings slightly raised, making a ‘V’ when seen head-on.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
Swamp Sparrow

A medium-sized sparrow with a short, conical bill, a compact, robust body, and a moderately long tail. The overall shape is quite similar to the widespread Song Sparrow.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
Yellow-rumped Warbler

Yellow-rumped Warblers are fairly large, full-bodied warblers with a large head, sturdy bill, and long, narrow tail.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
Wood Duck

Wood Ducks have a unique shape among ducks—a boxy, crested head, a thin neck, and a long, broad tail. In flight, they hold their head up high, sometimes bobbing it. Overall, their silhouette shows a skinny neck, long body, thick tail, and short wings.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
Broad-winged Hawk

Broad-winged Hawks are small, compact raptors with chunky bodies and large heads. In flight, their broad wings come to a distinct point. The tail is short and square.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
Red-tailed Hawk

Red-tailed Hawks are large hawks with typical Buteo proportions: very broad, rounded wings and a short, wide tail. Large females seen from a distance might fool you into thinking you’re seeing an eagle. (Until an actual eagle comes along.)

Media Player Error
Update your browser
Black-throated Green Warbler

The Black-throated Green Warbler is a medium-sized warbler similar in size and shape to many others in the Setophaga genus. Plump and seemingly large-headed, with a thick, straight bill and shortish tail.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
Baltimore Oriole

Smaller and more slender than an American Robin, Baltimore Orioles are medium-sized, sturdy-bodied songbirds with thick necks and long legs. Look for their long, thick-based, pointed bills, a hallmark of the blackbird family they belong to.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
Indigo Bunting

Indigo Buntings are small (roughly sparrow-sized), stocky birds with short tails and short, thick, conical bills. In flight, the birds appear plump with short, rounded tails.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
Wild Turkey

Wild Turkeys are very large, plump birds with long legs, wide, rounded tails, and a small head on a long, slim neck.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
Blue-headed Vireo

A stocky small songbird with medium-length tail and fairly heavy, hooked bill and thick legs (compared to warblers).

Media Player Error
Update your browser
Great Crested Flycatcher

Great Crested Flycatchers are large flycatchers with fairly long and lean proportions. Like many flycatchers they have a powerful build with broad shoulders and a large head. Despite its name, this bird’s crest is not especially prominent. The bill is fairly wide at the base and straight; the tail is fairly long.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
Palm Warbler

Palm Warblers are small songbirds, but they are on the larger side for a warbler and have a fuller looking belly. Their posture is more upright than a typical warbler and more like a pipit—especially noticeable when they are on the ground. Their tails and legs are longer than most warblers contributing to the pipitlike shape.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
Barn Swallow

When perched, the sparrow-sized Barn Swallow appears cone shaped, with a slightly flattened head, no visible neck, and broad shoulders that taper to long, pointed wings. The tail extends well beyond the wingtips and the long outer feathers give the tail a deep fork.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
Rose-breasted Grosbeak

Rose-breasted Grosbeaks are stocky, medium-sized songbirds with very large triangular bills. They are broad-chested, with a short neck and a medium-length, squared tail.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
Ruby-crowned Kinglet

Kinglets are tiny songbirds with relatively large heads, almost no neck, and thin tails. They have very small, thin, straight bills.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
Yellow-bellied Sapsucker

Yellow-bellied Sapsuckers are fairly small woodpeckers with stout, straight bills. The long wings extend about halfway to the tip of the stiff, pointed tail at rest. Often, sapsuckers hold their crown feathers up to form a peak at the back of the head.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
Purple Finch

Among the small forest birds like chickadees, kinglets, and nuthatches, Purple Finches are large and chunky. Their powerful, conical beaks are larger than any sparrow’s. The tail seems short and is clearly notched at the tip.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
Brown-headed Cowbird

Brown-headed Cowbirds are smallish blackbirds, with a shorter tail and thicker head than most other blackbirds. The bill has a distinctive shape: it’s much shorter and thicker-based than other blackbirds', almost finch-like at first glance. In flight, look for the shorter tail.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
Belted Kingfisher

Belted Kingfishers are stocky, large-headed birds with a shaggy crest on the top and back of the head and a straight, thick, pointed bill. Their legs are short and their tails are medium length and square-tipped.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
Hermit Thrush

Hermit Thrushes have a chunky shape similar to an American Robin, but smaller. They stand upright, often with the slender, straight bill slightly raised. Like other thrushes, the head is round and the tail fairly long.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
Prairie Warbler

Small songbird with thin, straight bill. Slender, with a long tail and long legs relative to other warblers.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
Killdeer

Killdeer have the characteristic large, round head, large eye, and short bill of all plovers. They are especially slender and lanky, with a long, pointed tail and long wings.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
Least Flycatcher

Least Flycatchers are the smallest Empidonax flycatcher in the East. Like other flycatchers they tend to perch upright, but they appear a little more compact than most. The primary feathers on their wings are short, which makes their thin tail look longer. Their head is round and is not peaked at the back of the head like some other Empidonax.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
Golden-crowned Kinglet

These are tiny songbirds with a rounded body, short wings, and skinny tail. They have relatively large heads, and their bills are short and thin, perfect for gleaning small insects.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
Pine Siskin

Pine Siskins are very small songbirds with sharp, pointed bills and short, notched tails. Their uniquely shaped bill is more slender than that of most finches. In flight, look for their forked tails and pointed wingtips.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
Common Merganser

These are large, long-bodied ducks with thin, pointed wings. Their bills are straight and narrow, unlike the wide, flat bill of a “typical” duck. Females have shaggy crests on the backs of their heads.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
Wood Thrush

The Wood Thrush's pot-bellied body, short tail, straight bill, big head, and upright posture give it the profile of a scaled-down American Robin.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
Bald Eagle

The Bald Eagle dwarfs most other raptors, including the Turkey Vulture and Red-tailed Hawk. It has a heavy body, large head, and long, hooked bill. In flight, a Bald Eagle holds its broad wings flat like a board.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
Bobolink

Bobolinks are small songbirds with large, somewhat flat heads, short necks, and short tails. They are related to blackbirds and orioles, and they have a similar shaped, sharply pointed bill.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
Savannah Sparrow

Savannah Sparrows are medium-sized sparrows with short, notched tails. The head appears small for the plump body, and the crown feathers often flare up to give the bird’s head a small peak. The thick-based, seed-eating bill is small for a sparrow.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
Hooded Merganser

Hooded Mergansers are small ducks with a thin bill and a fan-shaped, collapsible crest that makes the head look oversized and oblong. In flight, the wings are thin and the tail is relatively long and rounded.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
Ring-necked Duck

A compact diving duck with a distinctive head shape—a sloping forehead and peaked rear crown. The crown flattens when they are diving. In flight, Ring-necked Ducks appear large-headed with a thin neck and a short, round body.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
Eastern Towhee

Towhees are a kind of large sparrow. Look for their thick, triangular, seed-cracking bill as a tip-off they’re in the sparrow family. Also notice the chunky body and long, rounded tail.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
Alder Flycatcher

A small flycatcher with an upright posture and a large, usually rounded head. The bill is straight and fairly broad, and the wings are short.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
Double-crested Cormorant

Double-crested Cormorants are large waterbirds with small heads on long, kinked necks. They have thin, strongly hooked bills, roughly the length of the head. Their heavy bodies sit low in the water.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
Northern Parula

Northern Parulas are small wood-warblers with a short tail and a thin, pointy bill. They are plump little warblers about the size of a kinglet.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
Ring-billed Gull

The Ring-billed is a medium-sized gull with a fairly short, slim bill. When the gull perches, its long, slender wings extend well past its square-tipped tail. In flight, the birds move lightly on easy flaps of their fairly slender wings.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
Northern Rough-winged Swallow

Small, long-bodied bird with a small head and bill. Flies on long and relatively broad, pointed wings. Note square tail.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
Lincoln's Sparrow

The Lincoln's Sparrow is a medium-sized sparrow with a dainty appearance. It has a rounded belly and head, but the back of its head often looks pointed or crested when it raises its crown feathers. Its tail is relatively short and its conical bill is a bit thinner compared to most other sparrows.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
Common Nighthawk

Common Nighthawks are medium-sized, slender birds with very long, pointed wings and medium-long tails. Only the small tip of the bill is usually visible, and this combined with the large eye and short neck gives the bird a big-headed look.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
Black-throated Blue Warbler

Black-throated Blue Warblers are small, well-proportioned birds with sharp, pointed bills. Compared with other warblers, they are fairly large and plump.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
Cooper's Hawk

A medium-sized hawk with the classic accipiter shape: broad, rounded wings and a very long tail. In Cooper’s Hawks, the head often appears large, the shoulders broad, and the tail rounded.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
White-crowned Sparrow

The White-crowned Sparrow is a large sparrow with a small bill and a long tail. The head can look distinctly peaked or smooth and flat, depending on the bird’s attitude.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
Common Goldeneye

Common Goldeneyes are medium sized ducks with large heads. The bill is fairly small and narrow. It slopes smoothly downward from the face, giving the head an overall triangular shape. Goldeneyes are diving ducks with streamlined bodies and short tails.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
Spotted Sandpiper

The Spotted Sandpiper is a medium-sized shorebird with a bill slightly shorter than its head and a body that tapers to a longish tail. They have a rounded breast and usually appear as though they are leaning forward.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
Blackpoll Warbler

Blackpoll Warblers are small songbirds with a short tail and a small thin bill. Compared to other warblers they have longer wings.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
Osprey

Ospreys are very large, distinctively shaped hawks. Despite their size, their bodies are slender, with long, narrow wings and long legs. Ospreys fly with a marked kink in their wings, making an M-shape when seen from below.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
Magnolia Warbler

A small songbird with a small bill and a long, narrow tail.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
American Black Duck

American Black Ducks are large ducks with a profile nearly identical to Mallards. They have rounded heads, thick bills, and bulky bodies. Like other dabbling ducks they sit high in the water with their tails high.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
Field Sparrow

Field Sparrows are small, slender sparrows with relatively short, conical bills, rounded heads, and somewhat long tails.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
Brown Thrasher

Brown Thrashers are fairly large, slender songbirds with long proportions—the legs are long and sturdy, and the bill is long and slightly downcurved. The tail is long, too, and often cocked upward in the manner of wrens.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
Common Loon

Common Loons are large, diving waterbirds with rounded heads and dagger-like bills. They have long bodies and short tails that are usually not visible. In flight, they look stretched out, with a long, flat body and long neck and bill. Their feet stick out beyond the tail (unlike ducks and cormorants), looking like wedges.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
Red-shouldered Hawk

Red-shouldered Hawks are medium-sized, with broad, rounded wings and medium-length tails that they fan out when soaring. In flight, they often glide or soar with their wingtips pushed slightly forward, imparting a distinctive, “reaching” posture.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
Winter Wren

The Winter Wren is a plump round ball with a stubby tail that it usually holds straight up. Its bill is small and thin, in keeping with its diminutive appearance.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
American Kestrel

The slender American Kestrel is roughly the size and shape of a Mourning Dove, although it has a larger head; longer, narrow wings; and long, square-tipped tail. In flight, the wings are often bent and the wingtips swept back.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
Blackburnian Warbler

A typical medium-sized warbler with a short, thin, pointed bill, trim body, and medium-length tail. The overall shape is similar to the widespread Yellow Warbler.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
Louisiana Waterthrush

A large, somewhat plump warbler, with a stout bill (bigger than Northern Waterthrush’s), rather short tail, and long legs.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
Northern Waterthrush

A large but trim warbler with a short, fine bill, a rather short tail, and long legs. The bill is smaller than Louisiana Waterthrush’s.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
Blue-gray Gnatcatcher

Blue-gray Gnatcatchers are tiny, slim songbirds with long legs; a long tail; and a thin, straight bill.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
Fox Sparrow

Fox Sparrows are large, round-bodied sparrows with stout bills and medium-length tails.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
Solitary Sandpiper

A small, slender shorebird with a somewhat long neck and long bill. It has fairly long wings and legs.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
Red Crossbill

A stocky, medium-sized songbird with short, notched tail and an unusual, twisted bill that crosses when closed.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
Willow Flycatcher

Willow Flycatchers are small, slender flycatchers, but they are one of the larger members of the Empidonax genus. They have a fairly long, thin tail and wings. The bill is broad. Like other flycatchers, they tend to perch upright.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
Nashville Warbler

A small songbird, compact with a round head, plump body and shortish tail. The bill is fine, straight, and pointed.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
Sharp-shinned Hawk

Sharp-shinned Hawks are small, long-tailed hawks with short, rounded wings. They have small heads that in flight do not always project beyond the “wrists” of the wings. The tail tends to be square-tipped and may show a notch at the tip. Females are considerably larger than males.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
Fish Crow

Fish Crows fit the standard crow shape: hefty, well-proportioned birds with heavy bills, sturdy legs, and broad wings. At rest, Fish Crows’ wings fall short of their medium-length, square tails.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
Barred Owl

Barred Owls are large, stocky owls with rounded heads, no ear tufts, and medium length, rounded tails.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
Green Heron

Compared with most herons, Green Herons are short and stocky, with relatively short legs and thick necks that are often drawn up against their bodies. They have broad, rounded wings and a long, daggerlike bill. They sometimes raise their crown feathers into a short crest.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
Cape May Warbler

A delicate, short-tailed warbler with a slender and distinctively decurved bill, unusual among warblers.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
Bufflehead

Bufflehead are very small, compact ducks with large, rounded heads and short, wide bills.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
Yellow-billed Cuckoo

Yellow-billed Cuckoos are fairly large, long, and slim birds. The bill is almost as long as the head, thick and slightly downcurved. They have a flat head, thin body, and very long tail. Wings appear pointed and swept back in flight.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
Redpoll

A small songbird with a small head and small, pointed bill. The tail is short with a notch at the tip.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
Black-billed Cuckoo

Cuckoos are slender, dove-sized birds with a longer slightly curved bill and a very long tail. When perched it often has a hunchbacked posture.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
Green-winged Teal

Green-winged Teal are very small ducks. They have short, blocky bodies and their tails sit high out of the water. The head is large, the neck is short, and the bill is relatively small.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
Bank Swallow

A small swallow with a chunky body, large head, relatively short pointed wings, and a slightly forked tail.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
Tennessee Warbler

Small and stocky for a warbler, with a short tail and a thin and sharply pointed bill (markedly smaller and more pointed than a Red-eyed Vireo's bill).

Media Player Error
Update your browser
Least Sandpiper

Least Sandpipers are tiny sandpipers with round bodies and relatively short, pointed wings. They have thin-tipped, slightly decurved bills and slim, medium-length legs.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
Pink-footed Goose

A compact, medium-sized goose with a short, thick neck; rounded head; and short, triangular bill.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
Bay-breasted Warbler

A small songbird with a fine, pointed bill and rather long wings and tail.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
Merlin

Merlins are small falcons with a powerful build that is broader and stockier than the slightly smaller American Kestrel. Merlins have sharply pointed wings, a broad chest, and a medium length tail.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
Rusty Blackbird

Rusty Blackbird is a medium-sized blackbird with a slender bill and medium-length tail. The bill is slightly decurved. They are a bit larger and longer-tailed than Red-winged Blackbird with a more slender bill. Rusty Blackbird is thinner-billed and shorter-tailed than Common Grackle.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
Pine GrosbeakUncommon

The Pine Grosbeak is a large and plump, heavy-chested finch with a round head. Its bill is thick and conical, but much stubbier than in other finch species. The tail is long and slightly notched.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
Canada WarblerUncommon

Canada Warblers are small, well-proportioned birds with a straight, sharp bill. Compared to other warblers, they have a slightly longer tail with a fuller chest.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
Black-legged KittiwakeUncommon

A small gull with a plump belly. Kittiwakes look sleek in flight, with rather long, narrow wings, a short, notched tail, and a slim, fairly short bill.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
American PipitUncommon

A small, slim songbird with a short, thin bill, and medium-length tail, recalling a lark (or a sparrow, but lacking a sparrow’s heavier bill).

Media Player Error
Update your browser
Swallow-tailed KiteUncommon

Swallow-tailed Kites are large but slender and buoyant raptors. They have long, narrow, pointed wings, slim bodies, and a very long, deeply forked tail. The bill is small and sharply hooked.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
Grasshopper SparrowUncommon

Grasshopper Sparrow is on the small side for a sparrow, with a distinctive compact shape. The head is large and flat-crowned, with a conspicuous bill. Tail is very short.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
Great EgretUncommon

Great Egrets are tall, long-legged wading birds with long, S-curved necks and long, dagger-like bills. In flight, the long neck is tucked in and the legs extend far beyond the tip of the short tail.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
Say's PhoebeUncommon

This slender, long-tailed flycatcher appears large-headed for a bird of its size. The head often looks flat on top, but phoebes sometimes raise their head feathers into a small peak at the back.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
Northern HarrierUncommon

Northern Harriers are slender, medium-sized raptors with long, fairly broad wings and a long, rounded tail. They have a flat, owl-like face and a small, sharply hooked bill. Harriers often fly with their wings held in a dihedral, or V-shape above the horizontal.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
American CootUncommon

The American Coot is a plump, chickenlike bird with a rounded head and a sloping bill. Their tiny tail, short wings, and large feet are visible on the rare occasions they take flight.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
Eastern Whip-poor-willUncommon

Eastern Whip-poor-wills are medium-sized birds with a large, rounded head and a stout chest that tapers to a long tail and wings, giving them a distinctly front-heavy look.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
American WoodcockUncommon

American Woodcocks are plump, short-legged shorebirds with very long, straight bills. Their large heads, short necks, and short tails give them a bulbous look on the ground and in flight. The wings are broad and rounded compared to most other shorebirds.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
Vesper SparrowUncommon

Vesper Sparrows are fairly large sparrows with a chunky body, a fairly small conical bill, and a long, notched tail.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
Swainson's ThrushUncommon

Swainson’s Thrushes are medium-sized thrushes—slim songbirds with round heads and short, straight bills. Their fairly long wings and medium-length tail can make the back half of the bird appear long.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
Peregrine FalconUncommon

Peregrine Falcons are the largest falcon over most of the continent, with long, pointed wings and a long tail. Be sure to look at shape as well as size—long primary feathers give the Peregrine a long-winged shape. As with most raptors, males are smaller than females, so Peregrines can overlap with large female Merlins or small male Gyrfalcons.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
Ruffed GrouseUncommon

Ruffed Grouse are fairly small grouse with a short, triangular crest and a long, fan-shaped tail. They have short legs and often look slimmer than other grouse species.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
Snow BuntingUncommon

A medium-sized, full-bodied songbird with a small conical bill.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
Snow GooseUncommon

A medium-sized goose with a hefty bill and long, thick neck. Juveniles are slightly smaller than adults in the fall, and this can be noticeable in flocks during fall and early winter.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
Yellow-throated VireoUncommon

Yellow-throated Vireos are small songbirds, but they are chunky, with a big head, thick bill, and short tail.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
Wilson's WarblerUncommon

Wilson’s Warblers are one of our smallest warblers. They have long, thin tails and small, thin bills. They appear rather round bodied and large headed for their size.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
Philadelphia VireoUncommon

A small songbird; small for a vireo but still chunkier than a warbler. The bill is small compared with other vireos.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
Bohemian WaxwingUncommon

Bohemian Waxwings are full-bellied, thick-necked birds with a shaggy crest atop a pin head. The wings are broad and pointed, like a starling's. The tail is fairly short and square-tipped.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
Bullock's OrioleUncommon

Bullock's Orioles are medium-sized songbirds with slim but sturdy bodies and medium-long tails. Orioles are related to blackbirds and share their long, thick-based, sharply pointed bills.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
White-winged DoveUncommon

White-winged Doves are plump, square-tailed doves with relatively long, thin bills and small heads.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
Northern PintailUncommon

Northern Pintails are elegant, long-necked ducks with a slender profile. The tail is long and pointed, but it is much longer and more prominent on breeding males than on females and nonbreeding males. In flight, the wings are long and narrow.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
Pied-billed GrebeUncommon

Pied-billed Grebes are small, chunky swimming birds. They have compact bodies and slender necks, with relatively large, blocky heads and short, thick bills. They have virtually no tail.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
American Herring GullUncommon

American Herring Gulls are large gulls with hefty bills and robust bodies. In flight, they look barrel-chested and broad-winged compared to smaller species such as Ring-billed Gulls.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
American WigeonUncommon

American Wigeons are medium-sized, rather compact ducks with a short bill and a round head. They tend to sit on the water with their heads pulled down, giving them a no-necked look.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
Lesser ScaupUncommon

The Lesser Scaup is a medium-sized diving duck with a small peak at the back of the head. From the small peak, the back of the head and neck is flat, not rounded as it is on Greater Scaup.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
Greater YellowlegsUncommon

Large and lanky for a shorebird, with very long legs, a long neck, and a thick-based, slightly upturned bill.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
Horned LarkUncommon

Horned Larks are small, long-bodied songbirds that usually adopt a horizontal posture. They have short, thin bills, short necks, and rounded heads—the shape sometimes broken by two small “horns” of feathers sticking up toward the back of the head.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
American Golden-PloverUncommon

A medium-sized shorebird with a rather large head, slim neck, long, pointed wings, and relatively long legs. The bill is short, slender, and straight.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
Little Blue HeronUncommon

This is a fairly small heron with a slight body, slender neck, and fairly long legs. It has rounded wings, and a long, straight, spearlike bill that is thick at the base.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
Virginia RailUncommon

The Virginia Rail is a chickenlike marsh bird with a long, heavy bill and a short, upturned tail. Head on, the Virginia Rail looks thin, but from the side they look rather full-bodied; what biologists call a laterally compressed body.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
American BitternUncommon

American Bitterns are medium-sized herons with thick, compact bodies. They have shorter legs and thicker necks than typical herons and a slightly hunched posture. The daggerlike bill is long, straight, and sharply pointed. The wings are broad but the wingtips are somewhat pointed.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
Evening GrosbeakUncommon

Evening Grosbeaks are large, heavyset finches with very thick, powerful, conical bills. They have a thick neck, full chest, and relatively short tail.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
Semipalmated SandpiperUncommon

A very small, plump shorebird with a thin, straight, tubular bill. When folded, the wings are about the same length as the tail. The bill usually does not droop or turn downwards at the tip.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
Northern ShovelerUncommon

The aptly named Northern Shoveler has a shovel-shaped bill that quickly sets it apart from other dabbling ducks. It is a medium-sized duck that tends to sit with its rear a bit higher out of the water almost like its bill is pulling its front half down.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
Barrow's GoldeneyeUncommon

A small, stocky diving duck with a large, oddly shaped, oblong head. The bill is relatively small and the forehead relatively steep compared with a Common Goldeneye.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
Eastern MeadowlarkUncommon

Eastern Meadowlarks are chunky, medium-sized songbirds with short tails and long, spear-shaped bills. In flight, their rounded wings, short tails, and long bills help set them apart from other grassland songbirds.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
GadwallUncommon

Gadwall are about the same size as Mallards. Gadwall have a fairly large, square head with a steep forehead. The bill is noticeably thinner than a Mallard's. In flight, the neck is slightly thinner and the wings slightly more slender than a Mallard’s.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
Olive-sided FlycatcherUncommon

A stocky, barrel-chested flycatcher, with a large head and a heavy, long bill for its size. It has rather long wings that can make the tail look short.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
Western Cattle-EgretUncommon

Compared with other herons, Western Cattle-Egrets are noticeably small and compact. They have relatively short legs and a short thick neck. The straight, daggerlike bill is shorter and thicker than other herons. They have medium-length, broad, rounded wings.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
Northern WheatearUncommon

Small thrush-like bird with distinctive black-and-white tail pattern best seen in flight. Throughout most of range, breeding male is overall pale with gray back and buffy throat, black mask, and black wings. Breeding female is duller without black mask. Nonbreeding and immat ...

Media Player Error
Update your browser
Lesser YellowlegsUncommon

A slender and elegant, medium-sized shorebirds with long legs and a long neck. The bill is straight and thin, only slightly longer than the bird's head (shorter and straighter than Greater Yellowlegs' bill).

Media Player Error
Update your browser
DickcisselUncommon

Compact and sparrowlike, with a full chest, a large, thick bill, and a relatively short tail.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
CanvasbackUncommon

A large, big-headed diving duck with a gently sloping forehead and a stout neck. Its long bill meets the sloping forehead, creating a seamless look from the top of the crown to the tip of the bill. On the water it has an oval body and a short tail that gently slopes down into the water.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
Wilson's SnipeUncommon

Wilson’s Snipes are medium-sized, pudgy shorebirds with short, stocky legs. The bill is straight and very long (several times the length of the head). The head is rounded and the tail is short.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
Blue-winged TealUncommon

A small dabbling duck, a Blue-winged Teal is dwarfed by a Mallard and only a touch larger than a Green-winged Teal. Head is rounded and bill is on the large side.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
Yellow-bellied FlycatcherUncommon

A compact, small flycatcher with a big-eyed look and a big head that sometimes shows a peak at the rear. It has a short tail and a small but wide bill.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
Semipalmated PloverUncommon

A small, compact shorebird with a short, stubby bill, a round head, and large eyes. The body is plump, with a short neck and medium-length legs.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
Ruddy DuckUncommon

These are small, compact ducks with stout, scoop-shaped bills, and long, stiff tails they often hold cocked upward. They have slightly peaked heads and fairly short, thick necks.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
Snowy EgretUncommon

These are medium-sized herons with long, thin legs and long, slender, bills. Their long, thin neck sets the small head well away from the body.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
Black ScoterUncommon

A stocky, medium-sized seaduck with a short tail, rounded head, and a broad bill that does not slope up to the forehead, as the Surf Scoter's bill does. Male Black Scoters have a rounded knob at the base of the bill.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
Orchard OrioleUncommon

Orchard Orioles are slim songbirds, larger than warblers and vireos. They have medium-length tails, rounded heads, and a straight, sharply pointed bill.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
Snowy OwlUncommon

Snowy Owls are very large owls with smoothly rounded heads that rarely show ear tufts. The body is bulky, with dense feathering on the legs that makes the bird look wide at the base when sitting on the ground.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
White-winged CrossbillUncommon

A compact, medium-sized finch with slightly forked tail and heavy, crossed bill.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
Long-tailed DuckUncommon

Long-tailed Duck is a small, slender sea duck with a rounded head and a small bill. The male has long tail plumes most of the year.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
Green-tailed TowheeUncommon

Green-tailed Towhees are small but chunky songbirds with a big head, stocky body and longish tail. The bill is thick and sparrowlike. They are larger than most sparrows and have shorter tails than most other towhees.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
Lapland LongspurUncommon

Small sparrowlike bird. Overall quite compact, with large head and relatively short tail. Wings relatively long.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
Greater ScaupUncommon

The Greater Scaup is a medium-sized diving duck with a rounded head.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
Mute SwanUncommon

Mute Swans are very large waterfowl. They have heavy bodies, short legs, and a long, slender neck habitually held in a graceful S. The large, flat bill has a bulging knob at the base.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
Northern ShrikeUncommon

A fairly large songbird with a thick neck and a large, rounded head with a thick, hooked bill. It has short wings and a long, rounded tail.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
Surf ScoterUncommon

A medium-sized sea duck with sturdy neck, rather large head, and heavy, broad bill.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
Rufous HummingbirdUncommon

A fairly small hummingbird with a slender, nearly straight bill, a tail that tapers to a point when folded, and fairly short wings that don’t reach the end of the tail when the bird is perched.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
DunlinUncommon

A chunky, small shorebird with medium-length legs, a short neck, and a long bill that is curved toward the tip.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
Red-headed WoodpeckerUncommon

Red-headed Woodpeckers are medium-sized woodpeckers with fairly large, rounded heads, short, stiff tails, and powerful, spike-like bills.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
Northern Saw-whet OwlUncommon

These are very small owls with large, rounded heads that lack ear tufts.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
Eurasian WigeonUncommon

A medium-sized duck, with a rounded head, medium-sized bill, moderately long tail, and broad, pointed wings.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
White-eyed VireoUncommon

Small songbird with a thick, straight, and slightly hooked bill. It has a rather compact body and a thick neck.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
Acadian FlycatcherUncommon

Small and long-tailed, with a large, peaked head and a broad, flat bill. Sits with a distinctly upright posture. Wings are long for a flycatcher.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
Clay-colored SparrowUncommon

Clay-colored Sparrows are petite, trim sparrows with a small bill, slender body, and fairly long, notched tail. The small, slender shape of these and other Spizella sparrows is distinctive.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
Orange-crowned WarblerUncommon

Orange-crowned Warblers are small songbirds. Compared with other warblers, they have noticeably thin, sharply pointed bills. They have short wings and short, square tails.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
Mourning WarblerUncommon

A small but sturdy songbird with a fairly full body and thick neck. The bill is thin and straight, smaller than the bill of the similar-looking Connecticut Warbler. It has a moderately long tail and strong legs.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
Hudsonian WhimbrelUncommon

A robust, large shorebird with a very long, curved bill and relatively long neck and legs. The wings are long and pointed; the tail is short.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
Glossy IbisUncommon

A medium-sized wading bird with compact body, long neck, long legs, and long, curved bill.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
Yellow-headed BlackbirdUncommon

Yellow-headed Blackbirds are fairly large blackbirds, with a stout body, a large head, and a long, conical bill.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
Sandhill CraneUncommon

Sandhill Cranes are very large, tall birds with a long neck, long legs, and very broad wings. The bulky body tapers into a slender neck; the short tail is covered by drooping feathers that form a “bustle.” The head is small and the bill is straight and longer than the head.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
Bonaparte's GullUncommon

A small, ternlike gull, with slender bill, rather narrow, pointed wings, and slim body.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
Blue-winged WarblerUncommon

Blue-winged Warblers are small, well-proportioned birds with a sharp and pointed bill. Compared to other warblers, the rather heavy black bill and eyeline give them a pointy-headed look.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
Connecticut WarblerUncommon

A plump warbler with short tail, rather long, pink legs, large eye, and rather heavy, spikelike bill. The long undertail coverts almost reach the tip of the rather short tail, unlike in Mourning Warblers.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
SanderlingUncommon

Sanderlings are small, plump sandpipers with a stout bill about the same length as the head. These and other sandpipers in the genus Calidris are often called “peeps”; Sanderlings are medium-sized members of this group.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
Great Horned OwlUncommon

These are large, thick-bodied owls with two prominent feathered tufts on the head. The wings are broad and rounded. In flight, the rounded head and short bill combine to create a blunt-headed silhouette.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
Red-throated LoonUncommon

A large ducklike seabird with long, sinuous neck, a thin but daggerlike bill, and long, narrow, pointed wings. The legs are set far back on the body and the feet trail behind the tail in flight.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
American GoshawkUncommon

American Goshawks are large hawks and the largest and bulkiest of the accipiters. They have broad, rounded wings and long tails. Relatively long secondary flight feathers give the trailing edge of the wing a curved or bulging look; the wingtips can look pointed in flight. Females are larger than males.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
Caspian TernUncommon

A large, heavy-bodied seabird (and largest of all terns), with a large head; thick, straight, pointed bill; and shallowly forked tail. The long, pointed wings are broad for a tern.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
Cackling GooseUncommon

A compact, small goose, with short neck, rounded crown, rather long wings, and short legs. The small bill is short and meets the forehead at a steep angle.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
Great Gray OwlUncommon

Great Grays are one of our tallest owls, and have broad wings and a long tail. They are big headed owls with a large facial disk. They dwarf most other owls in size but not by weight; their bulk is mostly made of feathers. Females are larger than males.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
Pectoral SandpiperUncommon

A stout, medium-sized shorebird with a moderately long, fairly thick-based bill and long wings. Males are considerably larger and about 50% heavier than females.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
Marsh WrenUncommon

The Marsh Wren is a small, plump, round-bodied wren with a short tail that it often holds up almost vertically above its back. It has a thin bill and short wings.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
Lark SparrowUncommon

A large, long-tailed sparrow. When perched, it often looks long-bodied with a thin neck and a round head. The tail is long and rounded.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
Black VultureUncommon

Black Vultures are large raptors. In flight, they hold their broad, rounded wings flat and angled slightly forward. The tail is very short and rounded. They have small, bare heads and narrow but strongly hooked bills.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
White-winged ScoterUncommon

A stocky duck with a thick neck and a heavy head with a sloping forehead and a stout bill. Males have a knob at the base of the bill. The wings are rather broad. The tail is fairly long and may be held upward out of the water.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
Greater White-fronted GooseUncommon

A medium-sized, heavy-bodied goose with a small bill, thick legs, and a short neck.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
Cliff SwallowUncommon

These compact swallows have rounded, broad-based wings, a small head, and a medium-length, squared tail.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
Red-breasted MerganserUncommon

A large, slim, and long-bodied diving duck with a fairly long neck and a long, thin, serrated bill. A shaggy crest of feathers gives the head a distinctive profile.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
Horned GrebeUncommon

A small, compact waterbird with a compact body, relatively short neck, blocky head (sometimes peaked), and straight, narrow bill, very unlike a duck's bill.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
Black-crowned Night HeronUncommon

Black-crowned Night Herons are small herons with rather squat, thick proportions. They have thick necks, large, flat heads, and heavy, pointed bills. The legs are short and, in flight, barely reach the end of the tail. The wings are broad and rounded.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
Great CormorantUncommon

A heavyset seabird with a long, thick neck, blocky head, and heavy hooked bill. The legs are short, the tail fairly long, and the wings broad.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
Iceland GullUncommon

Iceland Gulls are medium-sized gulls with relatively slender, short bills. They have fairly long wings that extend well past the tail.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
Eastern Screech-OwlUncommon

The Eastern Screech-Owl is a short, stocky bird, with a large head and almost no neck. Its wings are rounded; its tail is short and square. Pointed ear tufts are often raised, lending its head a distinctive silhouette.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
Golden-crowned SparrowUncommon

These are large, long-tailed sparrows with small heads and short but stout, seed-eating bills.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
SoraUncommon

Soras are small, chubby, chickenlike birds with long toes. They have a stubby bill unlike other rails in the United States and Canada, which have longer bills. They frequently hold their short tail cocked up.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
Yellow-crowned Night HeronUncommon

Yellow-crowned Night Herons are fairly small herons with stocky bodies and short, thick necks, and short legs. They have large, blocky heads with thick, relatively short bills. In flight, the wings are broad and rounded.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
Red-necked GrebeUncommon

A thickset waterbird, similar in size to many ducks, but with a longer neck and a heavy, pointed bill.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
Purple MartinUncommon

Purple Martins are very large, broad-chested swallows. They have stout, slightly hooked bills, short, forked tails, and long, tapered wings.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
Sedge WrenUncommon

A diminutive, compact songbird with a tiny, sharp, curved bill; short wings; long, sturdy legs; and medium-length tail.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
Nelson's SparrowUncommon

A medium-sized sparrow with a short conical bill, plump body, and a rather short tail in which the individual feathers often appear spiky at the tips.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
Common TernUncommon

Common Terns have long, narrow, angular wings and pointed wingtips. Unlike gulls, this tern has a straight, slender bill. The tail is forked and the legs are short.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
Ruddy TurnstoneUncommon

The Ruddy Turnstone is a short, stocky, and oval-shaped shorebird. They have a stout and slightly upturned bill.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
Long-eared OwlUncommon

Long-eared Owls are medium-sized, slender owls with long ear tufts. The head, roughly as wide as it is long, looks squarish. The facial disks are long and narrow.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
Western TanagerUncommon

Western Tanagers are stocky songbirds; fairly small yet noticeably larger and heavier-bodied than warblers. They have short, thick-based bills and medium-length tails.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
Purple GallinuleUncommon

A chicken-sized rail with a heavy conical bill, short tail (often cocked), a compact body, and very long legs and toes.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
Townsend's SolitaireUncommon

This thrush is a medium-sized songbird with a long tail, a short bill, and a small rounded head relative to its body size. Their upright posture and long tail gives them a long and slender appearance.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
Anna's HummingbirdUncommon

Tiny among birds, Anna’s are medium-sized and stocky for a hummingbird. They have a straight, shortish bill and a fairly broad tail. When perched, the tail extends beyond the wingtips.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
Lazuli BuntingUncommon

Lazuli Buntings are small, finchlike, stocky songbirds with cone-shaped bills and gently sloping foreheads. The tail is notched or slightly forked.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
Cerulean WarblerUncommon

A small, compact warbler that often perches horizontally. It has a small, round bill and a shorter tail than some other warblers such as Magnolia Warbler.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
Worm-eating WarblerUncommon

A small songbird, shaped like many other warblers but with a rather long, spikelike bill and moderately long wings and tail.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
Golden-winged WarblerUncommon

Golden-winged Warblers are small songbirds with fairly slim bodies and short tails. They have a thin, straight bill that is sharply pointed.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
Buff-breasted SandpiperUncommon

A small, delicately built shorebird with a fairly long neck, rounded head, and large eye. The bill is slender and short for a sandpiper bill (about the length of the head). At rest, the wingtips are about the length of the tail. Legs are fairly long.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
Laughing GullUncommon

Laughing Gulls are medium-sized gulls with fairly long wings and long legs that impart a graceful look when they are flying or walking. They have stout, fairly long bills.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
Rough-legged HawkUncommon

Rough-legged Hawks are fairly large hawks with broad wings that, compared to other Buteo hawks, are fairly long and narrow. The tail is also longer than in many other buteos. The wingtips are broad and often swept back slightly from the wrist, giving a hint of an M shape to the wing. The bill is fairly small.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
Boreal ChickadeeUncommon

A small songbird with husky body and head, and a slim, rather long tail.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
Short-billed DowitcherUncommon

A chunky, medium-sized shorebird with a long, straight bill and moderately long legs.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
Yellow-breasted ChatUncommon

Chats are small songbirds but are larger and bulkier than warblers or vireos. They have a long tail, large head and a relatively thick, heavy bill.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
Black TernUncommon

A small and delicately built seabird with a thin, pointed bill; long, pointed wings; a shallowly forked tail; and short legs.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
Golden EagleUncommon

Golden Eagles are one of the largest birds in North America. The wings are broad like a Red-tailed Hawk's, but longer. At distance, the head is relatively small and the tail is long, projecting farther behind than the head sticks out in front.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
Short-eared OwlUncommon

Short-eared Owls are medium-sized owls with rounded heads. The "ears" mentioned in their name are difficult to see. The wings are broad and the tips are smoothly rounded. The tail is short.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
White-rumped SandpiperUncommon

Among the small “peep” sandpipers, this is a fairly large species with a fairly long, slightly drooping bill. It has very long wings, with the wingtips extending past the tail tip when folded.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
BrantUncommon

A compact, small goose with a stubby bill and a relatively short neck compared to most geese.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
Hooded WarblerUncommon

Hooded Warblers are small, well-proportioned birds with a straight, sharp bill. Compared to other warblers, they are fairly heavy-bodied and thick-necked.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
Great Black-backed GullUncommon

This is the largest species of gull in the world. They are stout-bodied, with broad wings, a thick neck, and a heavy, slightly bulbous bill.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
Blue GrosbeakUncommon

The Blue Grosbeak is a stocky songbird with a very large, triangular bill that seems to cover the entire front of its face, from throat to forehead.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
Western KingbirdUncommon

Western Kingbirds are fairly large flycatchers with large heads and broad shoulders. They have heavy, straight bills, long wings, and a medium-length, square-tipped tail.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
Common GallinuleUncommon

Common Gallinules are medium-sized marsh birds with long legs and toes. In profile, note the small head, thin neck, and small, thin bill. Swimming birds frequently hold their wings up, such that the wingtips stick up on the back.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
Lesser Black-backed GullUncommon

A medium to large gull with rather long, pointed wings (wingtips extend far beyond the tail when perched). Although smaller and slimmer than other “large gulls” like Herring Gull, this species has a larger head and thicker bill than “medium” gulls like Ring-billed.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
Bicknell's ThrushUncommon

A small thrush, with straight, rather short bill and medium-length tail.

Media Player Error
Update your browser
Upland SandpiperUncommon

A shorebird with unusual proportions: long legs, a long, thin neck, small dovelike head, large eye, and thin, straight bill. The tail and the wings are long.

Media Player Error
Update your browser

Bird ID on the Go

Our free app includes worldwide coverage, sound ID, photo ID, and more.

birding, and opportunities to help bird conservation.
